mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances following a >0.2pp UNRATE increase.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is declining, with a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of periods following CPIAUCSL monthly increases above 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product directly correlates with increased corporate revenue and profit growth, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side weakness and inventory drawdowns, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following an INDPRO decline greater than 0.5%.
